---
title: "README"
author: "Clara Jégousse"
date: "2026-03-31"
output: html_document
---

# tunaki

A Quarto report on tuna fisheries in Kiribati, built on FAO Global Capture
Production data retrieved via the [fishR](https://github.com/clarajegousse/fishR)
package.

## About

Kiribati sits at the heart of the world's most productive tuna grounds. Its
Exclusive Economic Zone spans 3.5 million km2 of the central Pacific and is
home to major stocks of skipjack, yellowfin, bigeye, and albacore tuna.
This report tracks historical and recent catch trends using FAO-reported
national statistics.

## Structure

| File | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `tunaki.qmd` | Main Quarto report |
| `R/colours.R` | IOC colour palette and species colour mapping |
| `R/theme.R` | Shared ggplot2 theme (theme_tunaki) |
| `R/data.R` | Data loading and filtering via fishR |

## Usage

### Requirements
```r
install.packages(c(
  "ggplot2", "dplyr", "scales",
  "sysfonts", "showtext",
  "sf", "mregions2",
  "rnaturalearth", "rnaturalearthdata",
  "knitr"
))

remotes::install_github("clarajegousse/fishR")
```

### Render
```bash
quarto render tunaki.qmd
```

This produces both HTML and Word outputs in `_output/`.

### Publish
```bash
quarto publish gh-pages
```

## Data sources

- FAO Global Capture Production:
  https://www.fao.org/fishery/en/collection/capture
- Maritime Boundaries Geodatabase (VLIZ):
  https://www.marineregions.org
- Natural Earth: https://www.naturalearthdata.com

## Related

- [fishR](https://github.com/clarajegousse/fishR) - R package for FAO
  fisheries data

## License

MIT

---

## TODO

### Map
- [ ] Add Pacific EEZ map highlighting Kiribati (lobster orange)
- [ ] Colour EEZ zones by yellowfin tuna catch intensity (gradient)
- [ ] Add PNA member states layer for context?

### Figures
- [ ] Scientific illustrations of tuna species (skipjack, yellowfin, bigeye, albacore) 
- [ ] Consider silhouettes via rphylopic as axis decorations or watermarks?
- [ ] Add a proportional area chart showing species share over time

### Data
- [ ] Cross-reference FAO data with WCPFC stock assessment reports??
- [ ] Add WCPFC catch estimates as a comparison layer (licensed foreign vessels)
- [ ] Check if Kiribati EEZ catch vs flag-reported catch differs significantly

### Report
- [ ] Add executive summary section
- [ ] Add methods section describing FAO data limitations

### fishR
- [ ] Add plot function for decadal averages to fishR
- [ ] Consider adding EEZ map helper to fishR or a separate spatial package
